page: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. This configuration delivers 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core on a shared CPU allocation model running at 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong showing in memory-intensive and single-core operations. The instance achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory testing. Large-block memory bandwidth is strong for both read and write operations, and single-threaded data compression and decompression tasks also rank in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance metrics, including stress-ng multi-core CPU and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, fall into the average category. This average tier performance is also observed in database operations, Redis SET operations, static web serving, and small-model LLM inference. A notable bottleneck is present in medium-model LLM prompt processing, which registers as weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high memory density relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require dedicated CPU allocations. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which explains why multi-core benchmarks remain average despite the top-tier single-core capabilities of the AMD EPYC Genoa architecture. This server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, medium-sized databases, and general-purpose web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over sustained multi-threaded compute performance.
description: The Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC Genoa processor with a shared CPU allocation running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance, complemented by strong large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving remain average, while medium-model LLM prompt processing is weak. This memory-optimized profile offers high resource density for memory-bound applications. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching systems, and database operations that benefit from high memory capacity rather than sustained multi-threaded compute.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd is an 8-core AMD EPYC Genoa instance with 64 GB RAM and 400 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining high-density RAM and fast NVMe storage for memory-bound workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 8 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs with 64 GB of system memory
Bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
